title: Brain Tumor Classification Using DenseNet-121
meta: Deep Learning + Transfer Learning + Explainable AI
short: Implemented transfer learning for three-class brain tumor classification.
bullets: Implemented transfer learning using DenseNet-121 for three-class brain tumor classification (meningioma, glioma, pituitary).
Trained and validated on 3,064 contrast-enhanced T1-weighted MRI images with stratified 80-20 split.
Achieved 93.5% best validation accuracy using Adam optimizer, outperforming RMSprop (90.7%).
Surpassed baseline CNN models (70-85% validation accuracy range).
Applied focal loss (γ = 2, α = 0.25), dropout (0.5), and systematic hyperparameter tuning for improved generalization.
Optimized batch size (10) and learning rate scheduling for stable convergence.
Integrated Grad-CAM to generate interpretable heatmaps highlighting tumor regions influencing classification decisions.
Evaluated performance using precision, recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC, and confusion matrix analysis.

title: Intelligent Odor Sensing and ML-Driven Perfume Dispensing System
short: Multi-sensor VOC detection and automated dispensing.
bullets: Designed multi-sensor electronic nose architecture for VOC detection.
Applied PCA-based dimensionality reduction reducing input dimensionality by 67.9% while retaining 99% variance.
Developed ML regression model achieving MAE of 0.04 mL on fragrance composition prediction.
Implemented normalization and calibration pipeline to mitigate sensor drift and improve prediction stability across samples.
Integrated Python ML pipeline with Arduino-based actuation system for automated volumetric dispensing.
Engineered complete sensing -> inference -> mechanical execution pipeline.

title: Machine Learning Assisted Vehicular Visible Light Communication (VLC)
short: LED–photodiode based VLC prototype for V2V communication.
bullets: Developing LED-photodiode based VLC prototype for vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication.
Modeling real-world channel impairments including ambient light interference, motion-induced distortion, and alignment variability.
Designing ML-based adaptive thresholding algorithms to improve signal detection reliability under dynamic conditions.
Extracting temporal and intensity-domain signal features for noise-resilient classification.
Conducting controlled hardware experiments to evaluate bit error rate (BER) performance.
Currently optimizing robustness and real-time inference performance for dynamic vehicular environments.
